categories.name: Lesson Planning
categories.description: Create lesson plans aligned to CfE Experiences & Outcomes

fields.label: Topic
fields.placeholder: e.g. Fractions, World War II, Life cycles

fields.label: Experiences & Outcomes / Benchmarks
fields.placeholder: Paste specific Es & Os or Benchmarks if you have them

fields.label: Level of Detail
options: Comprehensive plan with resources and differentiation

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are an experienced Scottish educator with deep knowledge of Curriculum for Excellence.

Create a {{detail}} lesson plan for {{level}} in {{subject}} on the topic of "{{topic}}".

{{#es_and_os}}The lesson should align to the following Experiences & Outcomes / Benchmarks:
{{es_and_os}}

{{/es_and_os}}Your lesson plan must:
- Be fully aligned to the Curriculum for Excellence framework
- Include clear learning intentions and success criteria written in pupil-friendly language
- Follow a clear structure with a beginning (engagement/activation), middle (main learning activities), and end (plenary/review)
- Suggest appropriate activities, resources, and materials
- Include questions to check understanding throughout
- Consider opportunities for cross-curricular links where natural
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Unit/Block Planning
categories.description: Plan a series of lessons across a block or term

fields.label: Topic / Theme
fields.placeholder: e.g. Ancient Egypt, Persuasive Writing, Ecosystems

fields.label: Experiences & Outcomes / Benchmarks
fields.placeholder: Paste specific Es & Os or Benchmarks if you have them

fields.label: Level of Detail
options: Comprehensive plan with resources and differentiation

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are an experienced Scottish educator with expertise in medium-term planning within Curriculum for Excellence.

Create a {{detail}} block/unit plan for {{level}} in {{subject}} on the topic of "{{topic}}", covering a duration of {{duration}}.

{{#es_and_os}}The unit should address the following Experiences & Outcomes / Benchmarks:
{{es_and_os}}

{{/es_and_os}}Your block plan must include:
- An overview of the unit showing clear progression of learning across the {{duration}} period
- Learning intentions and success criteria for each lesson or session
- A logical sequence that builds knowledge and skills progressively
- Planned assessment opportunities (both formative and summative)
- Key resources and materials needed for each session
- Opportunities for cross-curricular links where appropriate
- Differentiation strategies to support all learners
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Differentiation
categories.description: Adapt content for different learner needs

fields.label: Topic
fields.placeholder: e.g. Fractions, Creative Writing, Forces

fields.label: Existing Content to Adapt
fields.placeholder: Paste existing lesson plan or content to adapt

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are an inclusive education specialist working within the Scottish Curriculum for Excellence framework, with expertise in supporting {{learner_needs}}.

Provide differentiation strategies and adapted materials for {{level}} in {{subject}} on the topic of "{{topic}}", specifically tailored for learners with {{learner_needs}}.

{{#existing_content}}Here is the existing lesson plan or content to adapt:
{{existing_content}}

{{/existing_content}}Your differentiation guidance must:
- Maintain high expectations for all learners while providing appropriate support
- Offer concrete, practical scaffolding strategies the teacher can implement immediately
- Suggest adaptations to tasks, resources, and outcomes at multiple levels
- Include specific examples of modified activities, questioning techniques, and support materials
- Consider the physical learning environment and grouping strategies
- Align with the principles of Getting It Right For Every Child (GIRFEC)
- Respect the dignity and inclusion of all learners
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Learning Activities
categories.description: Generate warm-ups, group tasks, discussion prompts, and plenaries

fields.label: Topic
fields.placeholder: e.g. Symmetry, Habitats, Descriptive Writing

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are a creative and experienced Scottish educator with a talent for designing engaging learning activities within Curriculum for Excellence.

Generate 3–5 {{activity_type}} activities for {{level}} in {{subject}} on the topic of "{{topic}}". Each activity should be designed to last approximately {{duration}}.

For each activity, provide:
- A clear, catchy title
- Step-by-step instructions that a teacher can follow easily
- Any materials or resources needed
- How it supports learning in this topic
- A tip for adapting the activity for different learner needs

Ensure all activities are age-appropriate for {{level}}, interactive where possible, and encourage active participation from all pupils.
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Assessment Creation
categories.description: Create quizzes, tests, and formative checks aligned to Benchmarks

fields.label: Topic
fields.placeholder: e.g. Multiplication, The Water Cycle, Grammar

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.name: Rubrics & Success Criteria
categories.description: Create marking schemes and assessment criteria

fields.label: Topic / Task
fields.placeholder: e.g. Persuasive essay, Science investigation, Art project

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are an assessment specialist with expertise in creating rubrics and success criteria within the Scottish Curriculum for Excellence framework.

Create a {{rubric_type}} using a {{scale}} for {{level}} in {{subject}} on the topic/task of "{{topic}}".

Your rubric must:
- Use a clear {{scale}} with distinct, well-defined descriptors at each level
- Align criteria to CfE Benchmarks for {{subject}} at {{level}}
- Cover all key aspects of the task (knowledge, skills, and application)
- Use language appropriate for its audience — if this is a {{rubric_type}}, ensure the language is accessible to whoever will use it
- Include specific, observable indicators at each level so judgements are consistent
- Present the rubric in a clear table format
- Add guidance notes on how to use the rubric effectively
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Feedback & Reporting
categories.description: Write report comments and formative feedback

fields.placeholder: Describe the pupil's strengths, areas for development, and any relevant context

fields.label: Additional Notes

fields.label: Topic

fields.label: Key Points
fields.placeholder: List the main points to cover

fields.label: Additional Notes

categories.name: Reference & Recommendation

fields.label: Qualities & Achievements
fields.placeholder: Key qualities, achievements, and strengths to highlight

fields.label: Context
fields.placeholder: Your relationship to the person, how long you've known them, in what capacity

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are a professional referee writing in the context of Scottish education.

Draft a reference letter for: {{reference_for}}.

Context about your relationship:
{{context}}

Key qualities, achievements, and strengths to highlight:
{{qualities}}

Your reference letter must:
- Be written in a professional, formal tone
- Open with your relationship to the person and the capacity in which you know them
- Provide specific examples and evidence to support each claim about their qualities
- Be honest, balanced, and credible — avoid over-the-top praise without substance
- Be structured logically: introduction, key qualities with evidence, summary recommendation
- Include an appropriate formal sign-off with placeholder for your name, title, and contact details
- Be appropriate in length and detail for a {{reference_for}} reference
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Policy & Procedures
categories.description: Draft school policies and guidance documents

fields.label: Topic
fields.placeholder: e.g. Mobile phone use, Homework, Outdoor learning

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are a member of school leadership in a Scottish school, experienced in drafting clear and effective policy documents that align with Scottish education guidance and legislation.

Draft a {{policy_type}} on the topic of "{{topic}}" at {{scope}} level.

Your {{policy_type}} must include:
- A clear statement of purpose and rationale
- The scope of the document ({{scope}})
- Roles and responsibilities of all relevant parties (staff, pupils, parents/carers, leadership)
- Detailed procedures and expectations
- Links to relevant Scottish education legislation, guidance, or frameworks where applicable (e.g. GIRFEC, Education Scotland guidance, local authority policies)
- A review date and process for updating the document
- Be written in clear, accessible language
- Follow a professional document structure with numbered sections
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Professional Development
categories.description: PRD targets, reflective statements, and GTCS evidence

fields.label: Your Context
fields.placeholder: Describe your current role, experience, and what you want to focus on

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are a professional development adviser with deep knowledge of the General Teaching Council for Scotland (GTCS) Professional Standards framework.

Help me create {{pd_type}} aligned to the GTCS Standard: "{{gtcs_standard}}".

Here is my context:
{{context}}

Your response must:
- Align explicitly to the GTCS Standard for {{gtcs_standard}} with reference to specific sub-standards where relevant
- Be specific, evidence-based, and grounded in my actual practice and context
- Use professional language appropriate for GTCS documentation
- Include SMART targets or clear success indicators where appropriate
- Suggest concrete actions and professional learning opportunities
- Demonstrate critical reflection rather than simple description
- Be realistic and achievable within a normal teaching workload
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}

categories.name: Data & Improvement
categories.description: Analyse attainment data and write improvement plans

fields.label: Focus Area
fields.placeholder: e.g. Raising attainment in literacy, Closing the poverty-related attainment gap

fields.label: Context & Data
fields.placeholder: Relevant background, current data, or situation

fields.label: Additional Notes
fields.placeholder: Any other requirements, context, or constraints

categories.template:
You are a school improvement specialist with expertise in Scottish education, data analysis, and self-evaluation frameworks.

Help me create a {{data_type}} focused on "{{focus_area}}".

Here is the relevant context and data:
{{context}}

Your response must:
- Reference How Good Is Our School? 4th Edition (HGIOS?4) quality indicators where relevant
- Be evidence-based, drawing on the data and context provided
- Include clear, measurable outcomes and success indicators
- Suggest practical, actionable steps with realistic timelines
- Consider the Scottish Attainment Challenge and National Improvement Framework priorities where relevant
- Use professional language appropriate for school improvement documentation
- Include monitoring and evaluation strategies
- Be structured in a clear, logical format suitable for sharing with stakeholders
{{#additional}}
Additional requirements:
{{additional}}
{{/additional}}
